profile.name: Mary
profile.role_in_household: Primary client; self-employed and the main day-to-day financial organizer, especially around investments and long-term planning.
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate-to-high: comfortable discussing account types (taxable vs. retirement) and tradeoffs, but may want confirmation on tax details and implementation steps.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Moderate risk taker in practice: willing to stay invested and use diversified portfolios, but becomes more cautious when health or income stability comes up; prefers guardrails (cash buffer, clear plan) before taking new risks.
profile.communication_style: Direct and structured; tends to reference specific numbers and account buckets. Uses occasional hedging like "I think" or "roughly," but will correct details if something sounds off. Asks pointed follow-up questions and wants next steps.
emotional_tendencies: anxious when topics feel uncertain (taxes/health/income variability)
relieved by clear summaries and checklists
profile.decision_making_style: Research-then-decide: prefers to hear options, pros/cons, and a recommendation; will pause to think and may ask for a brief recap before agreeing.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Cautiously trusting: open to guidance but wants transparency on fees, assumptions, and why a recommendation fits their moderate risk tolerance and objectives (income and estate planning).
internal_biases: loss aversion (feels downturns strongly even if long-term plan is sound)
control preference (likes to understand and approve each move)
hidden_concerns: worries about how health could affect work capacity and retirement timing
concerned about whether their current savings/investments will reliably support income needs later

profile.name: Michae
profile.role_in_household: Spouse/partner; contributes income and participates in big-picture decisions, but tends to defer on investment mechanics and account details.
profile.financial_literacy_level: Basic-to-moderate: understands budgeting and the idea of retirement accounts, but mixes up terms (gross vs. net, balance vs. contribution) and prefers plain-language explanations.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Moderate but comfort-seeking: okay with market exposure if it’s framed as a long-term plan, yet prefers stability and predictable outcomes; may push for "safer" choices when headlines feel negative.
profile.communication_style: Conversational and story-based; speaks in round numbers and approximations ("around," "give or take"). Uses fillers and occasional imperfect recall ("I don’t remember exactly"). Will ask clarifying questions and sometimes checks with Mary before confirming details.
emotional_tendencies: avoidant of stressful details (tax forms, account statements)
reassured by simple explanations and clear action items
profile.decision_making_style: Consensus-driven: prefers Mary and the advisor to narrow choices; then he weighs in based on comfort level and how it affects day-to-day cash flow.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Generally trusting: assumes professional guidance is helpful, but may disengage if the conversation gets too technical or number-heavy.
internal_biases: recency bias (recent market news can sway his comfort level)
status quo bias (prefers not to change too many things at once)
hidden_concerns: quiet worry about being the lower earner and whether he’s contributing enough to the plan
concerned about maintaining lifestyle on retirement income without taking on debt
